title: Chapter 10 - RESTful API Basics
description: Quick checks on REST principles and HTTP methods.

questions.prompt: Which HTTP method is typically used to create a new resource?

explanations.overview: POST is commonly used to create new resources.

byOptionId.b: Correct — POST usually creates new resources.

questions.prompt: Which status code indicates that a resource was successfully created?

explanations.overview: 201 Created means a new resource was created on the server.

questions.prompt: Which HTTP method is defined as idempotent (performing it multiple times has the same effect)?

explanations.overview: PUT is idempotent because repeating the same request results in the same state.

questions.prompt: What does HTTP status code 204 No Content mean?

options.label: The request succeeded but there is no response body

explanations.overview: 204 indicates success but intentionally no body is returned (useful for DELETE/PUT responses).

byOptionId.a: Correct — 204 means success with no content.
